name: psocbios
what_this_is: F3 -- a minimal BIOS firmware that USES the INT-service mechanism (now that real-mode IVT INT/IRET works). It installs an INT 10h teletype handler (AH=0Eh -> write AL to the COM1 console) in the IVT, then prints a banner by calling INT 10h per character. Each char round-trips through real-mode interrupt delivery (push FLAGS:CS:IP, IVT vector to the F000 handler) + IRET. qemu runs the same -bios, so the gate verifies OUR BIOS routine + the delivery, not a qemu-provided BIOS.
non_vacuous: Asserts both traces RUN the INT 10h handler (pc=0x3c, the COM1 write) AND finish the banner (pc=0x2a).
zero_new_rtl_note: No new RTL: built entirely on the real-mode INT/IRET delivery (commit 6f07377) + the existing 16550 UART + string engine. Prints 'Ventium BIOS: INT 10h teletype up'.
description: Bare-metal F3 BIOS-firmware differential. Proves the app -> INT 10h -> handler -> IRET BIOS-service flow byte-identical to qemu-system-i386 -cpu pentium over all 472 retired instructions, per-record. First real use of the interrupt-service mechanism -- the path to a bootloader + DOS.
